# Chapter 12 — SE(3)-Equivariant Policy Preview

## Robotics context

3D point clouds from depth / LiDAR change under **rigid transforms**, but the physical
grasp direction or wrench should transform the same way.

A naive MLP on flattened coordinates must re-learn every rotated view.
**SE(3)-equivariant** layers bake in symmetry: rotate input → output rotates accordingly.

## What this demo shows

1. L-shaped 3D point cloud + target grasp vector in body frame
2. Training clouds with random rotations (data augmentation in SE(3))
3. **Naive MLP** — low error in canonical frame, fails on a held-out rotation
4. **Equivariant readout** — scalar weights on invariant radii, vector sum stays equivariant

## Failure cases

| Wrong idea | What breaks |
|------------|-------------|
| Flatten (x,y,z) coordinates | Rotation changes input ordering / frame |
| Augment without equivariance | Needs exponentially more data |
| Invariant-only network for vector targets | Cannot output 3D force directions equivariantly |
| Confuse invariance vs equivariance | Scalars vs vectors transform differently |

## Run

Requires PyTorch:

```bash
pip install -e ".[torch]"
python chapters/12_se3_equivariant_preview/demo.py
```
